ast.source: The time-zone in which the authoring date should be shown. Either your local time-zone (default), the author's time-zone during commit creation or UTC\xB100:00.

ast.name: innerHTML
ast.source:
Whitespace and newlines are interpreted as part of the document and in changes by default (hence not ignored). This makes the last line being shown as 'changed' when a new subsequent line is added, even if the previously last line's text is the same.
If you don't care about purely-whitespace changes (e.g. list nesting / quote indentation changes), then activating this will provide more meaningful change detection.
ast.target:
Whitespace and newlines are interpreted as part of the document and in changes by default (hence not ignored). This makes the last line being shown as 'changed' when a new subsequent line is added, even if the previously last line's text is the same.
If you don't care about purely-whitespace changes (e.g. list nesting / quote indentation changes), then activating this will provide more meaningful change detection.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: You will get a pop up to specify your message.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Available placeholders: {{date}} (see below), {{hostname}} (see below), {{numFiles}} (number of changed files in the commit) and {{files}} (changed files in commit message).
ast.target: Available placeholders: {{date}} (see below), {{hostname}} (see below), {{numFiles}} (number of changed files in the commit) and {{files}} (changed files in commit message).

ast.name: setDesc

ast.target: A script that is run using 'sh -c' to generate the commit message. May be used to generate commit messages using AI tools. Available placeholders: {{hostname}}, {{date}}.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Specify custom hostname for every device.
ast.target: Specify custom hostname for every device.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Automatically pull commits when Obsidian starts.
ast.target: Automatically pull commits when Obsidian starts.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Hunks are sections of grouped line changes right in your editor.
ast.target: Hunks are sections of grouped line changes right in your editor.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: This allows you to see your changes right in your editor via colored markers and stage/reset/preview individual hunks.
ast.target: This allows you to see your changes right in your editor via colored markers and stage/reset/preview individual hunks.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Show the author of the commit in the history view.
ast.target: Show the author of the commit in the history view.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Show the date of the commit in the history view. The {{date}} placeholder format is used to display the date.
ast.target: Show the date of the commit in the history view. The {{date}} placeholder format is used to display the date.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: On slower machines this may cause lags. If so, just disable this option.
ast.target: On slower machines this may cause lags. If so, just disable this option.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Milliseconds to wait after file change before refreshing the Source Control View.
ast.target: Milliseconds to wait after file change before refreshing the Source Control View.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Set the style for the diff view. Note that the actual diff in "Split" mode is not generated by Git, but the editor itself instead so it may differ from the diff generated by Git. One advantage of this is that you can edit the text in that view.
ast.target: Set the style for the diff view. Note that the actual diff in "Split" mode is not generated by Git, but the editor itself instead so it may differ from the diff generated by Git. One advantage of this is that you can edit the text in that view.

ast.source: "Commit-and-sync" and "pull" takes care of submodules. Missing features: Conflicted files, count of pulled/pushed/committed files. Tracking branch needs to be set for each submodule.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source:
Sets the relative path to the vault from which the Git binary should be executed.
             Mostly used to set the path to the Git repository, which is only required if the Git repository is below the vault root directory. Use "\\" instead of "/" on Windows.
ast.target:
Sets the relative path to the vault from which the Git binary should be executed.
